content.text: North Carolina operates an online tool that translates a parcel dataset from a county to a standard set of fields for the same geometry. All 100 counties participate.

- Matches county fields to standard fields
- Produces a transformed data layer
- Boundaries are unchanged
- Also creates a point dataset—same fields
- Creates a metadata record
- Saves translation models for next update

- Statewide parcels
- Web services—many formats
- Downloadable data
- Refreshed weekly
- **Web application** – view parcels

Contacts:
Jeff Brown, NC Center for Geographic Information and Analysis jeff.brown@nc.gov

Pam Carver, and John Bridgers
Working Group for Seamless Parcels
pcarver@hendersoncountync.org jbridgers@sosnc.gov

Find and get parcels and other statewide geographic data:
NC OneMap Geospatial Portal
http://data.nconemap.gov

- Site address
- Mailing address
- Owner name
- Parcel assessed value
- Building value
- Land value
- Number of structures
- Structure present
- Multiple structures present
- Parcel identification number
- Deed book/page
- Deed date
- Acres (GIS and deeded)
- Subdivision
- Parcel use
- Sale date
- Transformed date

content.description: A map showing parcel boundaries in North Carolina with labels for Lenoir, Jones, Duplin, and Onslow counties.
